title: WebSocket Close Status Code
description: Schema describing WebSocket close status codes as defined in RFC 6455 Section 7.4. These codes indicate the reason a WebSocket connection was closed.

oneOf.title: Normal Closure
oneOf.description: The connection has fulfilled its purpose and is being closed normally.

oneOf.title: Going Away
oneOf.description: The endpoint is going away, such as a server shutting down or a browser navigating away.

oneOf.title: Protocol Error
oneOf.description: The endpoint is terminating the connection due to a protocol error.

oneOf.title: Unsupported Data
oneOf.description: The endpoint received a type of data it cannot accept (e.g., text-only endpoint receiving binary).

oneOf.title: No Status Received
oneOf.description: Reserved. Indicates no status code was present in the close frame. Must not be set by an endpoint.

oneOf.title: Abnormal Closure
oneOf.description: Reserved. Indicates the connection was closed abnormally without a close frame. Must not be set by an endpoint.

oneOf.title: Invalid Frame Payload Data
oneOf.description: The endpoint received data within a message that was not consistent with the type (e.g., non-UTF-8 data in a text message).

oneOf.title: Policy Violation
oneOf.description: The endpoint received a message that violates its policy.

oneOf.title: Message Too Big
oneOf.description: The endpoint received a message that is too big to process.

oneOf.title: Mandatory Extension
oneOf.description: The client expected the server to negotiate one or more extensions but the server did not.

oneOf.title: Internal Server Error
oneOf.description: The server encountered an unexpected condition that prevented it from fulfilling the request.

oneOf.title: TLS Handshake Failure
oneOf.description: Reserved. Indicates the TLS handshake failed. Must not be set by an endpoint.

reason.description: A human-readable reason string explaining the closure. Must not exceed 123 bytes when UTF-8 encoded.
